__TR_index_54__: Flags when 3 customers represent 60%+ of MRR. If any of those churn post-close, your "SaaS" becomes a professional services firm overnight. Index scored, not just flagged.

__TR_index_60__: A composite A–F grade weighting retention, concentration, expansion revenue, and growth trends. One letter grade tells you more than a 50-page CIM about whether this MRR will still be here in 12 months.

__TR_index_63__: The 3 false beliefs that cost buyers $340K
__TR_index_64__: If you're evaluating a SaaS right now, you probably hold at least one of these. Here's why each one is wrong.
__TR_index_66__: "The seller's churn methodology is industry-standard, so the number is reliable."
__TR_index_68__: There is no standard. Every seller makes methodology choices that flatter their number. The only reliable number is the one you compute from raw data. (4.2x average gap between reported and real.)
__TR_index_70__: "I'm not technical enough to analyze raw subscription data myself."
__TR_index_72__: You don't need to be. You need the CSV and a tool that computes churn five ways. The analysis takes minutes, not a finance degree. Your "I'm not technical" belief is exactly what sellers count on.
__TR_index_74__: "The seller wouldn't hide churn — they'd get caught in the earnout."
__TR_index_76__: Most churn methodology choices are legal, defensible, and invisible. Earnouts don't protect you from legitimate-looking accounting that understates real churn by 4x. The earnout catches fraud, not methodology.
